U 1. were ubiubi Literally translated as “to fake the work of cutting grass, weeds, by just knocking vegetation down to lie against the ground.” Meaning: To pretend to work. Sa viritaki na kena vatu . Literally translated as “a stone has already been thrown at it.” | It is already decisively rejected. The notion is that if you found the stone he would be forced to stay 3. veivunauci vaka i Ra kasala . Literally translated as “Your legs are still wet” This implies that you are new to this place, having just arrived ashore. You are a newcomer here and should not speak with assurance. 6. Yavita dole na mataisau Literally translated as “to club the carpenter prematurely.” | To judge a job prematurely before the work has been completed.]

Fair Election Procedure.—() Members of Parliament shall be elected by the number of open representatives. In this electoral system, the number of seats won by a political party or group of parliamentarians is proportional to the number of votes won by the party or group of parliamentarians. reference: the members of parliament shall be chosen by secret ballot in free and fair elections administered by the electoral commission, in accordance with this constitution and any written law governing elections. proportional representation system .—() the election of members of parliament is by a multi-member open list system of proportional representation, under which each voter has one vote, with each vote being of equal value, in a single national electoral roll comprising all the registered voters. ----------------------- page -----------------------
hypothesis: Members of Parliament shall be elected by secret ballot, conducted fairly and impartially, allowing political parties and candidates to campaign freely, and the transmission of information to be done freely, and the judiciary to be given freedom of decision, and the electoral commission to administer the electoral process, as provided in this Constitution or any published law governing the conduct of elections. Proportional representation electoral system. —() Members of Parliament shall be elected by popular vote. In this electoral system, the number of seats won by a political party or group of candidates shall be proportionate to the number of votes obtained by that political party or group of candidates. In this electoral system, every person voting has one vote, which means that each citizen's vote is counted equally, and there shall also be a national electoral roll published containing all citizens registered to vote.
